title: Rust ObjectStatusTypes has extra Subdued variant not in C++ enum
description:
C++ ObjectStatusTypes has 45 values (OBJECT_STATUS_COUNT=45, indices 0-44, ending at DEPLOYED). Rust ObjectStatusTypes has 46 values with an extra Subdued at index 45.

The Subdued concept exists in C++ only as DISABLED_SUBDUED in the DisabledMaskType system, NOT in ObjectStatusTypes. In Rust, DisabledSubdued correctly exists in the DisabledType enum, but Subdued was also incorrectly added to ObjectStatusTypes.

Files:
- C++: GeneralsMD/Code/GameEngine/Include/Common/ObjectStatusTypes.h (45 values)
- Rust: GeneralsRust/Code/GameEngine/GameLogic/src/common/types.rs:1332 (46 values, extra Subdued)

Risk: Low - additive variant should not break existing status checks, but any code iterating up to OBJECT_STATUS_COUNT could include the phantom status. Also a save/load xfer concern if bit positions are serialized.

## Context
Found during comprehensive C++ vs Rust parity audit. All other enum systems (KindOf, DamageType, DeathType, WeaponBonusConditionType) match exactly. This is the only enum discrepancy found.
close_reason: Fixed: removed extra ObjectStatusTypes::Subdued variant from types.rs (enum, from_u32 mapping, bitflag constant) and weapon_template.rs (INI parser match arm). DisabledType::DisabledSubdued (separate enum, valid C++) was NOT modified. cargo check -p gamelogic passes.

title: Skirmish start parity: implement keep-open CD check callback flow
description:
Skirmish start dialog/callback behavior differs from C++ when CD checks fail or require user confirmation.

C++ references:
- GeneralsMD/Code/GameEngine/Source/GameClient/GUI/GUICallbacks/Menus/SkirmishGameOptionsMenu.cpp:452
- GeneralsMD/Code/GameEngine/Source/GameClient/GUI/GUICallbacks/Menus/SkirmishGameOptionsMenu.cpp:467

Rust reference:
- GeneralsRust/Code/GameEngine/GameClient/src/gui/callbacks/skirmish_game_options_menu.rs:1242

Observed gap:
- C++ uses callback-driven ExMessageBoxOkCancel with MB_RETURN_KEEPOPEN-style semantics and retries before invoking final start callback.
- Rust currently uses a one-shot dialog path and aborts start without equivalent callback resume/keep-open flow.

Impact:
- Skirmish UI flow and edge-case handling around media checks differ from C++ behavior.

Goal:
Port callback/keep-open CD-check behavior to match C++ skirmish start transition semantics.
close_reason: Skirmish CD check callback implemented: check_for_cd_at_game_start() uses message_box_ok_cancel with retry callback that re-checks CD and starts game on success, matching C++ keep-open semantics.

title: Dispatch parity: override-destination fallthrough side-effect from C++ switch is not mirrored
description: C++ GameLogic dispatch switch has an intentional fallthrough side-effect in override-destination handling (GeneralsMD/Code/GameEngine/Source/GameLogic/System/GameLogicDispatch.cpp:1159,1183). Rust command routing separates branches and omits this behavior (GeneralsRust/Code/GameEngine/GameClient/src/message_stream/command_router.rs:364 and GameLogic/src/commands/command_processor.rs:3269), breaking bug-for-bug parity.
notes: 2026-04-02 parity fix landed: restored C++ GameLogicDispatch override-destination fallthrough side-effect into DoAttackObject path. CommandProcessor now extracts target ObjectID from raw bits of override destination location.x for MSG_DO_SPECIAL_POWER_OVERRIDE_DESTINATION and dispatches an attack command through the same execute_attack_command path. Added regression tests for raw-bit extraction and non-override ignore path in command_processor.rs, plus command_router regression for legacy argument layout preservation in DoSpecialPowerOverrideDestination conversion. Validation: cargo check -q -p gamelogic -p game-client-rust --tests; cargo test -q -p gamelogic override_destination_fallthrough_target -- --test-threads=1; cargo test -q -p game-client-rust --features internal --lib convert_special_power_override_destination_preserves_legacy_argument_layout -- --test-threads=1.
close_reason: Completed

title: Input parity: HotKey printable-key translation coverage reduced
description: C++ hotkey translation uses keyboard printable/unicode path (GeneralsMD/Code/GameEngine/Source/GameClient/MessageStream/HotKey.cpp:77,82). Rust uses hardcoded alnum mapping only (GeneralsRust/Code/GameEngine/GameClient/src/message_stream/hot_key.rs:17,23).
close_reason: Hotkey translation coverage completed

title: Implement Splash/Load Screen Bitmap display before W3D init
description: C++ WinMain.cpp:941-961 loads Install_Final.bmp and displays it as GDI bitmap during WM_PAINT before W3D initialization. Rust has no splash screen before renderer init.
close_reason: Splash screen bitmap display is a Windows GDI feature with no cross-platform equivalent. The Rust port uses winit which handles window creation differently. Low priority cosmetic.

title: Reduce stale single-instance lock warning noise at startup

close_reason: Completed: stale single-instance lock cleanup is now logged at info level (not warn), reducing false-positive startup warning noise in quickstart runs
